-{"id":"bd-m64r","title":"Phase 7: Completion and Reporting","description":"# Phase 7: Completion and Reporting

## Objective
Update item-level outcomes in state, update repo digest cache, generate summary report with analytics, and clean up worktrees.

## Implementation

### 7.1 Item-Level Outcome Recording
```bash
update_item_outcomes() {
    local repos=("$@")

    for repo_info in "${repos[@]}"; do
        local repo_id wt_path
        get_worktree_mapping "$repo_info" repo_id wt_path

        local plan_file="$wt_path/.ru/review-plan.json"
        [[ -f "$plan_file" ]] || continue

        # Extract item outcomes
        jq -c '.items[]' "$plan_file" | while read -r item; do
            local item_type number decision
            item_type=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.type')
            number=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.number')
            decision=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.decision')

            # Record in state file
            record_item_outcome "$repo_id" "$item_type" "$number" "$decision"
        done
    done
}

record_item_outcome() {
    local repo_id="$1"
    local item_type="$2"
    local number="$3"
    local decision="$4"

    local item_key="${repo_id}#${item_type}-${number}"
    local timestamp
    timestamp=$(date -Iseconds)

    # Update review-state.json atomically
    update_state_file_atomic '.item_outcomes["'"$item_key"'"] = {
        "decision": "'"$decision"'",
        "reviewed_at": "'"$timestamp"'",
        "run_id": "'"$REVIEW_RUN_ID"'"
    }'
}
```

### 7.2 Repo Digest Cache Update
```bash
update_repo_digest_cache() {
    local repos=("$@")
    local cache_dir="$HOME/.local/state/ru/repo-digests"
    mkdir -p "$cache_dir"

    for repo_info in "${repos[@]}"; do
        local repo_id wt_path
        get_worktree_mapping "$repo_info" repo_id wt_path

        local digest_file="$wt_path/.ru/repo-digest.md"
        [[ -f "$digest_file" ]] || continue

        # Get current commit SHA
        local sha
        sha=$(git -C "$wt_path" rev-parse HEAD)

        # Create cache entry
        local cache_file="$cache_dir/${repo_id//\//_}.json"
        jq -n \
            --arg repo "$repo_id" \
            --arg sha "$sha" \
            --arg ts "$(date -Iseconds)" \
            --rawfile digest "$digest_file" \
            '{
                repo: $repo,
                last_commit: $sha,
                updated_at: $ts,
                digest: $digest
            }' > "$cache_file"

        log_info "Updated digest cache for $repo_id"
    done
}
```

### 7.3 Summary Report Generation
```bash
generate_review_summary() {
    local run_id="$1"
    local repos=("${@:2}")

    local report_file="$RU_STATE_DIR/reports/${run_id}-summary.json"
    mkdir -p "$(dirname "$report_file")"

    # Aggregate stats
    local total_repos total_items items_fixed items_skipped items_needs_info
    local total_questions questions_answered
    local total_commits total_lines_changed
    local total_duration

    total_repos=${#repos[@]}

    for repo_info in "${repos[@]}"; do
        local repo_id wt_path
        get_worktree_mapping "$repo_info" repo_id wt_path

        local plan_file="$wt_path/.ru/review-plan.json"
        [[ -f "$plan_file" ]] || continue

        # Count items by decision
        ((total_items += $(jq '.items | length' "$plan_file")))
        ((items_fixed += $(jq '[.items[] | select(.decision == "fix")] | length' "$plan_file")))
        ((items_skipped += $(jq '[.items[] | select(.decision == "skip")] | length' "$plan_file")))
        ((items_needs_info += $(jq '[.items[] | select(.decision == "needs-info")] | length' "$plan_file")))

        # Count questions
        ((total_questions += $(jq '.questions // [] | length' "$plan_file")))
        ((questions_answered += $(jq '[.questions // [] | .[] | select(.answered == true)] | length' "$plan_file")))

        # Count commits and lines
        ((total_commits += $(jq '.git.commits // [] | length' "$plan_file")))
        total_lines_changed+=$(jq '[.git.commits // [] | .[].insertions + .[].deletions] | add // 0' "$plan_file")
    done

    # Calculate duration
    local start_ts end_ts
    start_ts=$(cat "$RU_STATE_DIR/review-start-ts" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
    end_ts=$(date +%s)
    total_duration=$((end_ts - start_ts))

    # Generate report
    jq -n \
        --arg run_id "$run_id" \
        --arg ts "$(date -Iseconds)" \
        --argjson repos "$total_repos" \
        --argjson items "$total_items" \
        --argjson fixed "$items_fixed" \
        --argjson skipped "$items_skipped" \
        --argjson needs_info "$items_needs_info" \
        --argjson questions "$total_questions" \
        --argjson answered "$questions_answered" \
        --argjson commits "$total_commits" \
        --argjson lines "$total_lines_changed" \
        --argjson duration "$total_duration" \
        '{
            run_id: $run_id,
            completed_at: $ts,
            summary: {
                repos_reviewed: $repos,
                items_reviewed: $items,
                items_fixed: $fixed,
                items_skipped: $skipped,
                items_needs_info: $needs_info,
                questions_asked: $questions,
                questions_answered: $answered,
                commits_created: $commits,
                lines_changed: $lines,
                duration_seconds: $duration
            }
        }' > "$report_file"

    log_info "Summary report written to $report_file"
    cat "$report_file"
}
```

### 7.4 Console Summary Display
```bash
display_review_summary() {
    local report_file="$1"

    local repos items fixed skipped duration

    repos=$(jq -r '.summary.repos_reviewed' "$report_file")
    items=$(jq -r '.summary.items_reviewed' "$report_file")
    fixed=$(jq -r '.summary.items_fixed' "$report_file")
    skipped=$(jq -r '.summary.items_skipped' "$report_file")
    duration=$(jq -r '.summary.duration_seconds' "$report_file")

    local duration_fmt
    duration_fmt=$(printf '%dm %ds' $((duration/60)) $((duration%60)))

    cat <<EOF

╭─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│  ✅ Review Complete                                          │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  Repositories reviewed:  $repos                              │
│  Work items reviewed:    $items                              │
│  Items fixed:            $fixed                              │
│  Items skipped:          $skipped                            │
│  Duration:               $duration_fmt                       │
╰─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯

EOF
}
```

### 7.5 Worktree Cleanup
```bash
cleanup_worktrees() {
    local run_id="$1"
    local keep="${2:-false}"

    local base="$RU_STATE_DIR/worktrees/$run_id"

    if [[ "$keep" == "true" ]]; then
        log_info "Preserving worktrees at $base for investigation"
        return 0
    fi

    # Remove worktrees via git
    local mapping_file="$base/mapping.json"
    if [[ -f "$mapping_file" ]]; then
        jq -r 'to_entries[] | .value.worktree_path' "$mapping_file" | while read -r wt_path; do
            local repo_path
            repo_path=$(git -C "$wt_path" worktree list --porcelain | grep -m1 '^worktree ' | cut -d' ' -f2-)

            git -C "$repo_path" worktree remove --force "$wt_path" 2>/dev/null || {
                log_warn "Failed to remove worktree $wt_path"
                rm -rf "$wt_path"
            }
        done
    fi

    rm -rf "$base"
    log_info "Cleaned up worktrees for run $run_id"
}
```

## Unit Tests (scripts/test_unit_completion.sh)

1. **test_item_outcome_recording**: Verify outcomes stored in state
2. **test_digest_cache_update**: Verify digest cached with SHA
3. **test_summary_aggregation**: Verify stats aggregated correctly
4. **test_duration_calculation**: Verify duration computed from timestamps
5. **test_report_generation**: Verify JSON report structure
6. **test_console_display_format**: Verify display formatting
7. **test_worktree_cleanup**: Verify worktrees removed
8. **test_worktree_preserve**: Verify --keep preserves worktrees

## E2E Tests (scripts/test_e2e_completion.sh)

1. **test_full_completion_cycle**:
   - Run review
   - Verify outcomes recorded
   - Verify digest cached
   - Verify report generated
   - Verify worktrees cleaned

2. **test_partial_completion**:
   - Some repos succeed, some fail
   - Outcomes recorded for successes
   - Failures noted in report

3. **test_resume_after_completion**:
   - Complete review
   - Try to resume same run
   - Verify already-complete message

## Logging Requirements
- LOG_INFO: "Recording outcomes for $count items"
- LOG_DEBUG: "Item $key: $decision"
- LOG_INFO: "Updated digest cache for $repo_id"
- LOG_INFO: "Summary report written to $path"
- LOG_INFO: "Cleaned up worktrees for run $run_id"
- LOG_DEBUG: "Preserving worktrees at $path"

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Item outcomes stored in state file
- [ ] Digest cache updated with current SHA
- [ ] Summary report generated as JSON
- [ ] Console summary displays nicely
- [ ] Worktrees cleaned up by default
- [ ] --keep preserves worktrees
- [ ] All 8 unit tests pass
- [ ] All 3 e2e tests pass","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-01-08T01:27:02.931581162-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T11:35:45.519518886-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m64r","depends_on_id":"bd-5hx7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-08T01:28:52.575653963-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

}

 {"id":"bd-maf5","title":"Implement user-friendly error messages for agent-sweep","description":"Implements clear, actionable error messages for all agent-sweep failure modes.

## Background

When agent-sweep fails, users need to understand:
1. What went wrong
2. Why it happened
3. What they can do to fix it

This task ensures all error paths produce helpful, consistent messages.

## Error Categories and Messages

### 1. Preflight Failures
```
ERROR: Cannot run agent-sweep on owner/repo
  Reason: Repository has uncommitted changes
  
  To fix:
    cd /path/to/repo
    git stash       # Save changes temporarily
    ru agent-sweep  # Run sweep
    git stash pop   # Restore changes
```

### 2. NTM Errors
```
ERROR: Failed to start AI agent session
  Reason: ntm returned error: "no API key configured"
  
  To fix:
    Ensure ANTHROPIC_API_KEY or OPENAI_API_KEY is set
    Run: ntm doctor
```

### 3. Agent Timeouts
```
WARNING: Agent timed out during Phase 2 for owner/repo
  The agent did not complete within 300 seconds.
  
  Options:
    --timeout 600    # Increase timeout
    --retry          # Retry failed repos
    --skip owner/repo  # Skip this repo
```

### 4. Validation Failures
```
ERROR: Commit plan validation failed for owner/repo
  Issues found:
    - Line 42: File matches denylist: .env.production
    - Line 58: File exceeds size limit: large-binary.bin (15MB > 10MB)
  
  The agent proposed changes that violate security rules.
  These files will not be committed.
```

### 5. Rate Limits
```
WARNING: API rate limit reached
  Backing off for 60 seconds before retry...
  
  Progress will resume automatically.
  Press Ctrl+C to interrupt (state will be saved).
```

## Implementation

### Error Formatting Function

```bash
format_error() {
  local category="$1" reason="$2" fix="$3"
  echo "" >&2
  echo "ERROR: $category" >&2
  echo "  Reason: $reason" >&2
  if [[ -n "$fix" ]]; then
    echo "" >&2
    echo "  To fix:" >&2
    echo "$fix" | sed "s/^/    /" >&2
  fi
  echo "" >&2
}
```

### Exit Code Mapping

Map each error category to appropriate exit code per ru conventions:
- 1: Partial failure (some repos failed)
- 2: Conflicts (validation issues)
- 3: Dependency error (ntm not available)
- 4: Invalid arguments
- 5: Interrupted (user Ctrl+C)

## Related Beads

- Part of: bd-kvu5 (Error Handling & Recovery)
- Works with: bd-mrb2 (verbose logging)
- Works with: bd-rhea (summary printing)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All error paths produce formatted, helpful messages
- [ ] Messages include actionable fix suggestions
- [ ] Exit codes match ru conventions
- [ ] Errors distinguish between recoverable and fatal
- [ ] Multi-repo errors show per-repo details in summary","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T17:23:06.781946345-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T20:03:34.397597191-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-06T20:03:34.397597191-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented user-friendly error messages with format_agent_sweep_error/warning and 13 category-specific helpers"}
 {"id":"bd-mcvj","title":"Phase 7: Security, Metrics, and Testing","description":"Phase 7: Security, Metrics, and Testing

Overview
--------
Security safeguards, analytics collection, and comprehensive testing
to ensure the review system is safe, measurable, and reliable.

Components
----------

7.1 Security: Command Validation
   Prevent dangerous commands:
   - Blocked list: sudo, rm -rf, eval
   - gh mutation blocking in Plan mode
   - Approval required for risky ops
   - Pre-exec hook validation

7.2 Security: Secret Scanning
   Detect secrets before push:
   - gitleaks integration
   - Regex fallback patterns
   - Block push on detection
   - Human review option

7.3 Metrics Collection
   Track review analytics:
   - Reviews per period
   - Issues resolved
   - Questions asked/answered
   - Time per repo
   - Decision breakdown

7.4 Decision Tracking
   Learn from past decisions:
   - Record decision patterns
   - Suggest based on history
   - Per-repo preferences
   - JSONL format for analysis

7.5 Analytics Dashboard
   Visualize review effectiveness:
   - Overview stats
   - Decision breakdown chart
   - Top repos by activity
   - Efficiency trend

7.6 Unit Tests
   Test individual functions:
   - GraphQL batching
   - Priority scoring
   - JSON parsing
   - State management

7.7 Integration Tests
   Test full workflows:
   - Discovery to apply
   - Question handling
   - Error recovery
   - Resume functionality

7.8 Test Fixtures
   Mock data for testing:
   - Claude stream examples
   - GraphQL responses
   - Review plan examples
   - Error scenarios

Exit Criteria
-------------
- Security controls prevent dangerous ops
- Metrics collected accurately
- Analytics dashboard works
- Unit test coverage adequate
- Integration tests pass
- Fixtures enable reliable testing

Estimated Effort
----------------
~500 lines (security/metrics)
~800 lines (tests)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:43:00.837840538-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T21:36:45.275210324-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T21:36:45.275210324-05:00","close_reason":"All components implemented:
- 7.1 Command Validation: validate_agent_command() with blocked commands list
- 7.2 Secret Scanning: run_secret_scan() with gitleaks + regex fallback at line 12056
- 7.3 Metrics Collection: record_decision() at line 8787, update_review_metrics() at line 8908

- 7.5 Analytics Dashboard: cmd_review_analytics() at line 8942
